title: Minuartia viscosa
taxonomy: Minuartia viscosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Minuartia, which consists of small to medium-sized annual or perennial plants. Minuartia viscosa is further classified as part of the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Caryophyllaceae is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. Minuartia viscosa's classification reflects its close relationship with other plants in the Caryophyllaceae family.
uses: Minuartia viscosa has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the mountainous regions of Europe, for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's sticky or viscid leaves are believed to have antimicrobial effects, making it useful for wound care and skin issues. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, Minuartia viscosa is thought to have antiseptic properties, which may help prevent infection in minor cuts and scrapes. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Minuartia viscosa for medicinal purposes.

parts: Minuartia viscosa, also known as sticky chickweed, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
